data.title: Owlet launches Dream Sock™ in South Africa, a medical device for monitoring infants' vital signs, promising parents peace of mind.
data.description: In Johannesburg, Owlet, Inc. revealed the debut of the Dream Sock™ in South Africa. As a leader in smart infant monitoring, Owlet introduces this innovative product which is certified as a Class C Medical Device with CE Marking. The Dream Sock™ allows parents to monitor their baby's live vital signs and receive immediate health alerts, offering enhanced peace of mind. Specifically tailored for infants aged between 0 to 18 months and weighing 2.5-13.6 kg, this product aims to provide reliable monitoring and assurance to caregivers.

data.title: Investor Hakimian warns U.S. economy under Trump's 2nd term is struggling with growth from government spending, tech expansion.
data.description: Hedge fund manager Spencer Hakimian has raised concerns about the state of the U.S. economy during President Donald Trump's second term. In a recent post, Hakimian described the economy as "barely hanging on by a string" six months into "Trump 2.0", attributing any growth to significant government spending increasing by over 10% annually, as well as the unrestricted expansion of big tech companies. He highlighted that the economy is mainly being driven by Washington DC and San Francisco, with other regions and sectors facing pressure. Hakimian emphasized the reliance on government spending and big tech, noting the challenges faced by other sectors. He also mentioned that the country is witnessing unchecked growth in these areas, indicating potential risks.
